Smokey Party Jollof Rice: A legendary, parboiled long-grain rice dish slow-cooked in an intense, smoky tomato, red bell pepper, and onion reduction base, deeply infused with bay leaves, thyme, white pepper, and local stock, traditionally scorched at the bottom of a cast-iron pot for its signature firewood aroma.
Seafood Okra Soup: A vibrant, viscous, and highly nutritious coastal delicacy made with finely chopped or grated fresh okra pods simmered in a rich seafood stock, loaded with jumbo prawns, calamari, crabs, snails, and fresh fish, typically finished with a light touch of palm oil and fresh scent leaves.
Gourmet Asun: A fiery, traditional Yoruba street food favorite consisting of tender, bite-sized cuts of bone-in goat meat that are thoroughly smoke-grilled, chopped, and vigorously tossed in a searing, coarse stir-fry of crushed red scotch bonnet peppers (atarodo) and onions.
Beef Suya: The ultimate northern Nigerian street food delicacy, featuring thin, ribbon-like strips of lean beef flank skewered and flash-grilled over open hardwood coals, heavily coated in 'yaji'—a complex, spicy dry rub made from ground peanuts, ginger, chili powder, and local spices.
Pounded Yam and Efo Riro: A classic luxury pairing featuring 'Iyan'—a smooth, stretchy, dough-like swallow made by vigorously pounding boiled indigenous white yams—served alongside a rich, textured wild spinach stew cooked in an aromatic pepper reduction with locust beans (iru) and assorted meats.
Egusi Soup: A ubiquitous, richly textured soup made from ground melon seeds that are protein-curded and simmered in a savory palm oil base with shredded bitterleaf or fluted pumpkin leaves (ugu), stockfish, dry fish, and various animal proteins.
Ofada Rice and Ayamase Stew: An aromatic, unpolished, short-grain indigenous rice paired with a notoriously spicy, dark-green designer stew made entirely from bleached palm oil, green scotch bonnets, green bell peppers, and fermented locust beans, densely packed with bite-sized 'shaki' (tripe) and cow skin.
Ewa Aganyin: An intensely buttery, slow-boiled brown bean mash cooked until completely soft, served with a signature dark, almost black, gritty sauce made from caramelized onions, dried crushed chili flakes, and deeply smoked palm oil.
Amala and Abula: A deeply cultural Yoruba combination featuring a soft, dark swallow made from dried yam flour (Amala), flooded with 'Abula'—a tri-component mixture of velvety bean soup (Gbegiri), jute leaf soup (Ewedu), and a fiery, rich pepper sauce laced with assorted meats.
Assorted Meat Pepper Soup: A light, intensely aromatic, and watery broth brewed with a fiery blend of scotch bonnets and native spices like calabash nutmeg (erhe) and uda pods, heavily loaded with mixed tripe, liver, kidney, and beef chunks, designed to clear the sinuses.
Moi Moi: A smooth, savory steamed bean pudding made from peeled black-eyed peas blended with fresh red bell peppers, onions, and scotch bonnets, enriched with palm oil or vegetable oil, and traditionally layered with boiled eggs, flaked fish, or corned beef.
Bole and Grilled Fish: A beloved South-South street food combo comprising sweet, caramel-rimmed ripe plantains (or savory unripe ones) roasted slowly over open charcoal grills, served with a whole spiced mackerel fish and a robust, spicy palm oil-based pepper and onion dipping sauce.
Nkwobi: A traditional Igbo evening lounge delicacy consisting of tenderly cooked, chopped cow feet dynamicly tossed and emulsified in a thick, rich, warm palm oil and potash sauce, garnished with bitter utazi leaves, fresh onions, and ground crayfish.
Edikang Ikong: A premium, dry, and highly nutritious soup native to the Efik/Ibibio people, expertly crafted from a precise, heavy ratio of fresh waterleaf and fluted pumpkin leaves (ugu), slow-simmered with practically no added water, reliant entirely on the juices of periwinkles, stockfish, and rich meats.
Banga Soup and Starch: A luxurious Delta-style soup extracted from the rich juice of fresh palm fruit nuts, slow-cooked with indigenous spices like beletete leaves and aidan fruit, served alongside 'Starch'—a smooth, elastic, orange-hued swallow made from cassava starch melted with palm oil.
Afang Soup: A rich, hearty, and intensely texturized ceremonial soup from Akwa Ibom, made from finely shredded wild Afang leaves (okazi) pounded together with waterleaf, simmered in heavy palm oil with periwinkles, smoked fish, stockfish, and dynamic local spices.
Gizdodo: A highly colorful, savory contemporary side dish or small chop featuring equal parts tender, bite-sized boiled-and-fried chicken gizzards and sweet fried plantain cubes (dodo), tossed together in a rich, sweet-and-spicy bell pepper, onion, and tomato stir-fry reduction.
Fried Yam and Akara: A classic Nigerian weekend breakfast comfort food pairing crispy, deep-fried white yam batons with light, airy, golden bean cakes made from a seasoned paste of whipped black-eyed peas, onions, and scotch bonnets, served with a hot pepper dipping sauce.
Ogbono Soup: A smooth, slippery, and highly aromatic draw soup made from ground wild mango seeds (dika nuts) dissolved in hot palm oil, slow-simmered with stockfish, local spices, and leafy greens like ugu, typically paired with fufu or pounded yam.
Abacha (African Salad): A vibrant, refreshing Eastern Nigerian delicacy made from dried, shredded cassava roots that are rehydrated and thoroughly tossed in a rich, warm, orange-hued palm oil and potash emulsion, flavored with ehuru, ugba (oil bean seeds), garden eggs, and topped with fried fish.
NOK by Alara: NOK by Alara is an upscale, contemporary African and Afro-fusion fine dining restaurant in Victoria Island featuring innovative twists on traditional West African dishes, set within a visually striking art gallery indoor space and an elegant, bamboo-framed garden terrace.
Yellow Chilli: Yellow Chilli is a premier gourmet traditional Nigerian and pan-African restaurant celebrated for its premium presentation of native delicacies, legendary party Jollof rice, seafood okra soup, and fiery local pepper soups with efficient delivery options.
Terra Kulture Food Lounge: Terra Kulture Food Lounge is a culturally immersive heritage hub and restaurant that serves authentic, generous portions of indigenous Nigerian staples like pounded yam, efo riro, and village rice in an artistic, celebratory local atmosphere.
Ile Eros: Ile Eros is an elegant, high-end modern Nigerian fine dining restaurant led by Chef Eros, specializing in haute-cuisine interpretations of classic street foods, refined local soups, and premium catering experiences.
Ofada Boy: Ofada Boy is a vibrant, casual cultural restaurant heavily focused on local street food heritage, serving massive value-for-money portions of native Ofada rice in leaves, spicy peppered goat meat asun, and fresh palm wine with excellent delivery app infrastructure.
Z Kitchen: Z Kitchen is an upscale, premium contemporary steakhouse and fine dining restaurant in Victoria Island featuring expert wood-fired grills, dry-aged beef steaks, global Mediterranean-inspired cuisine, premium wines, and a sleek modern architectural interior.
SLoW Lagos: SLoW Lagos is a sophisticated, high-end farm-to-table restaurant located at Temple Muse, offering global comfort food, Latin American flavors, wood-fired cooking, and artisanal mixology within a lush, botanical oasis indoor-outdoor setting.
Shiro Lagos: Shiro Lagos is a luxury, high-end Pan-Asian and Japanese restaurant and beachfront lounge featuring premium sushi, sashimi, dim sum, and spicy wok-tossed Singapore noodles, set against dramatic, towering interior architecture and ocean views.
Danfo Bistro: Danfo Bistro is a quirky, casual urban eatery celebrating everyday Lagos street culture, uniquely styled around the iconic yellow transit bus and serving quick-service local sliders, Ewa Aganyin variations, and street food favorites.
Glover Court Suya: Glover Court Suya is a legendary, long-standing local open-air kitchen dedicated entirely to authentic street-style grilled meats, serving perfectly spiced beef suya, ram suya, and kidneys tossed in intense traditional yaji dry pepper blends.
Blackbell: Blackbell is a popular, high-speed fast-casual multi-cuisine restaurant serving reliable, budget-friendly, and large portions of everyday local staples, smoky Jollof rice, quick pasta boxes, and fresh pastries built for rapid daily delivery.
Craft Gourmet by Lou Baker: Craft Gourmet by Lou Baker is a chic, cozy boutique cafe and bistro specializing in fresh artisanal pastries, Mediterranean-inspired light lunches, comforting weekend brunches, specialty coffees, and healthy vegetarian-friendly options.
The House Lagos: The House Lagos is a unique social house, cocktail lounge, and continental restaurant styled like a chic residential home, emphasizing shared group platters, interactive dinner parties, and a high-energy evening nightlife crowd.
RSVP Lagos: RSVP Lagos is a high-end, minimalist American-style upscale bistro and cocktail lounge featuring sleek industrial architecture, house-made pastas, premium gourmet beef burgers, custom mixology, and a sophisticated poolside dining terrace.
Ocean Basket VI: Ocean Basket VI is a popular, casual international seafood restaurant chain specializing in massive, shared family-style platters of fried or grilled fish, prawns, calamari, chips, and signature garlic sauces with high delivery efficiency.
Izanagi: Izanagi is an intimate, premier Japanese restaurant known for its consistent, high-quality execution of authentic sushi rolls, fresh sashimi, hot teppanyaki grills, and a peaceful, zen-inspired dining ambiance.
La Veranda: La Veranda is a sophisticated, elegant fine dining Italian restaurant serving classic handmade wood-fired pizzas, rich pasta dishes, slow-cooked risottos, and fine wines overlooking a quiet, romantic poolside terrace.
Cactus Restaurant: Cactus Restaurant is a massive, iconic waterfront family restaurant and bakery featuring a sprawling outdoor terrace facing the lagoon, an enormous international menu, fresh desserts, and dedicated kid-friendly play spaces.
Rora Lagos: Rora Lagos is a high-energy luxury Japanese-Mediterranean fusion restobar and nightlife hotspot featuring sleek gold-accented decor, premium sushi platters, vibrant fusion sliders, creative signature cocktails, and late-night resident DJs.
The Ona: The Ona is an exclusive, progressive chef-owned boutique restaurant celebrating avant-garde Nigerian gastronomy with innovative multi-course tasting menus, molecular culinary experimentation, and rare local ingredient reductions at an intimate chef's table.
